attributes.description: Disables the dropdown so the panel will not open.
attributes.name: disabled

attributes.description: The distance in pixels from which to offset the panel away from its trigger.
attributes.name: distance

attributes.description:
Indicates whether or not the dropdown is open. You can toggle this attribute to show and hide the dropdown, or you
can use the `show()` and `hide()` methods and this attribute will reflect the dropdown's open state.
attributes.name: open

attributes.description:
The preferred placement of the dropdown panel. Note that the actual placement may vary as needed to keep the panel
inside of the viewport.
attributes.name: placement

attributes.description: The distance in pixels from which to offset the panel along its trigger.
attributes.name: skidding

attributes.description:
By default, the dropdown is closed when an item is selected. This attribute will keep it open instead. Useful for
dropdowns that allow for multiple interactions.
attributes.name: stay-open-on-select

attributes.description: Syncs the popup width or height to that of the trigger element.
attributes.name: sync

cssParts.description: The component's base wrapper, an `<syn-popup>` element.
cssParts.name: base

cssParts.description: The popup's exported `popup` part. Use this to target the tooltip's popup container.
cssParts.name: base__popup

cssParts.description: The panel that gets shown when the dropdown is open.
cssParts.name: panel

cssParts.description: The container that wraps the trigger.
cssParts.name: trigger

events.description: Emitted after the dropdown closes and all animations are complete.
events.name: syn-after-hide

events.description: Emitted after the dropdown opens and all animations are complete.
events.name: syn-after-show

methods.description:
Instructs the dropdown menu to reposition. Useful when the position or size of the trigger changes when the menu
is activated.
methods.name: reposition

properties.description:
The dropdown will close when the user interacts outside of this element (e.g. clicking). Useful for composing other
components that use a dropdown internally.
properties.name: containingElement

properties.description: Disables the dropdown so the panel will not open.
properties.name: disabled

properties.description: The distance in pixels from which to offset the panel away from its trigger.
properties.name: distance

properties.description:
Indicates whether or not the dropdown is open. You can toggle this attribute to show and hide the dropdown, or you
can use the `show()` and `hide()` methods and this attribute will reflect the dropdown's open state.
properties.name: open

properties.description:
The preferred placement of the dropdown panel. Note that the actual placement may vary as needed to keep the panel
inside of the viewport.
properties.name: placement

properties.description: The distance in pixels from which to offset the panel along its trigger.
properties.name: skidding

properties.description:
By default, the dropdown is closed when an item is selected. This attribute will keep it open instead. Useful for
dropdowns that allow for multiple interactions.
properties.name: stayOpenOnSelect

properties.description: Syncs the popup width or height to that of the trigger element.
properties.name: sync

slots.description: The dropdown's trigger, usually a `<syn-button>` element.
slots.name: trigger

summary: Dropdowns expose additional content that "drops down" in a panel.
